user_message: Can you work with me on security posture for Pacific Northwest Outfitters?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Pacific Northwest Outfitters: there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us

handoffs.condition: Customer 360 unavailable, Kusto BI fallback needed

required_artifacts: SL4-level forecast rollup with source attribution

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF31-K trap (Rigid Customer-360-first policy fails when BI is known to have fresher data); produces complete SL4-level forecast rollup with source attribution, hierarchy alignment, period boundary reconciliati
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF31-K failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF31-K failure mode (Rigid Customer-360-first policy fails when BI is known to have fresher data)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct security posture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF31-K -- Rigid Customer-360-first policy fails when BI is known to have fresher data
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
